metadata.forced_mapping_note: Engramia runs natively on this harness — no forced mapping. Embedding backend: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (local). Every scenario uses the real public API (learn, recall, refine_pattern) and the real eval store path.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern (repeated quality observations update eval-weighted ranking)
results.pass_rule: After 10 iterations with 0% misleading-feedback noise, top-1 must be the ground-truth best approach on at least 80% of tasks.
notes: Random baseline is 1/3 (three candidates per task).
Noise: 0 of 120 feedback observations were misleading.

curves.convergence_interpretation: Fraction of 12 tasks whose current top-1 is the ground-truth best approach, sampled after each of 10 training iterations (index 0 = pre-training baseline). A growing curve indicates feedback accumulates; a flat/declining curve indicates noise dominates.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern (repeated quality observations update eval-weighted ranking)
results.pass_rule: After 10 iterations with 20% misleading-feedback noise, top-1 must be the ground-truth best approach on at least 80% of tasks.
notes: Random baseline is 1/3 (three candidates per task).
Noise: 24 of 120 feedback observations were misleading.

curves.convergence_interpretation: Fraction of 12 tasks whose current top-1 is the ground-truth best approach, sampled after each of 10 training iterations (index 0 = pre-training baseline). A growing curve indicates feedback accumulates; a flat/declining curve indicates noise dominates.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern (repeated quality observations update eval-weighted ranking)
results.pass_rule: After 8 iterations with 40% misleading-feedback noise, top-1 must be the ground-truth best approach on at least 80% of tasks.
notes: Random baseline is 1/3 (three candidates per task).
Noise: 23 of 96 feedback observations were misleading.

curves.convergence_interpretation: Fraction of 12 tasks whose current top-1 is the ground-truth best approach, sampled after each of 8 training iterations (index 0 = pre-training baseline). A growing curve indicates feedback accumulates; a flat/declining curve indicates noise dominates.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern downgrade demotes deprecated patterns out of top-K
results.pass_rule: ≥80% of top-5 matches are from the non-deprecated set (failed eval_score=0.5, good eval_score=7.0).
notes: Shared task text eliminates similarity variance so the scored signal is purely the quality multiplier.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of the top-K recalled matches that belong to the non-deprecated set, for K in {1, 3, 5, 10}. Degradation at higher K indicates the quality multiplier discriminates well at the head but loses resolution deeper in the list.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern downgrade demotes deprecated patterns out of top-K
results.pass_rule: ≥80% of top-5 matches are from the non-deprecated set (failed eval_score=3.0, good eval_score=7.0).
notes: Shared task text eliminates similarity variance so the scored signal is purely the quality multiplier.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of the top-K recalled matches that belong to the non-deprecated set, for K in {1, 3, 5, 10}. Degradation at higher K indicates the quality multiplier discriminates well at the head but loses resolution deeper in the list.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern downgrade demotes deprecated patterns out of top-K
results.pass_rule: ≥80% of top-5 matches are from the non-deprecated set (failed eval_score=4.0, good eval_score=6.0).
notes: Shared task text eliminates similarity variance so the scored signal is purely the quality multiplier.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of the top-K recalled matches that belong to the non-deprecated set, for K in {1, 3, 5, 10}. Degradation at higher K indicates the quality multiplier discriminates well at the head but loses resolution deeper in the list.

results.pass_rule: Average of (old wins at recency_weight=0, new wins at recency_weight=1) ≥ 80% with old-180d / quality 9.0 vs 7.0.
notes: Large limit (100) used so recency re-rank sees both cohorts; a small limit hides the competing cohort from the re-rank stage.

curves.sharpness_interpretation: Fraction of probes whose top-1 comes from the new (fresh) cohort as a function of recency_weight. A sharp step function near a specific weight indicates the knob flips ranking cleanly at that point; a smooth ramp indicates the knob blends the two cohorts gradually. Competitors without a recency knob cannot produce either shape — their fraction stays flat.

results.pass_rule: Average of (old wins at recency_weight=0, new wins at recency_weight=1) ≥ 80% with old-60d / quality 8.5 vs 7.5.
notes: Large limit (100) used so recency re-rank sees both cohorts; a small limit hides the competing cohort from the re-rank stage.

curves.sharpness_interpretation: Fraction of probes whose top-1 comes from the new (fresh) cohort as a function of recency_weight. A sharp step function near a specific weight indicates the knob flips ranking cleanly at that point; a smooth ramp indicates the knob blends the two cohorts gradually. Competitors without a recency knob cannot produce either shape — their fraction stays flat.

results.pass_rule: Average of (old wins at recency_weight=0, new wins at recency_weight=1) ≥ 80% with old-15d / quality 8.0 vs 7.5.
notes: Large limit (100) used so recency re-rank sees both cohorts; a small limit hides the competing cohort from the re-rank stage.

curves.sharpness_interpretation: Fraction of probes whose top-1 comes from the new (fresh) cohort as a function of recency_weight. A sharp step function near a specific weight indicates the knob flips ranking cleanly at that point; a smooth ramp indicates the knob blends the two cohorts gradually. Competitors without a recency knob cannot produce either shape — their fraction stays flat.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern quality boost + recency_weight on fresh cohort
results.pass_rule: ≥60% of top-5 come from v3 (10 patterns) despite v2 (20) being 2× more populous; v3 refined to eval=9.5.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of top-K matches that come from the fresh v3 cohort (refined quality + fresh timestamp) despite v2 being more populous. Drop-off at higher K shows how far the fresh cohort's ranking advantage extends.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern quality boost + recency_weight on fresh cohort
results.pass_rule: ≥60% of top-5 come from v3 (10 patterns) despite v2 (30) being 3× more populous; v3 refined to eval=8.5.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of top-K matches that come from the fresh v3 cohort (refined quality + fresh timestamp) despite v2 being more populous. Drop-off at higher K shows how far the fresh cohort's ranking advantage extends.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern quality boost + recency_weight on fresh cohort
results.pass_rule: ≥60% of top-5 come from v3 (10 patterns) despite v2 (40) being 4× more populous; v3 refined to eval=8.0.

curves.precision_at_k_interpretation: Fraction of top-K matches that come from the fresh v3 cohort (refined quality + fresh timestamp) despite v2 being more populous. Drop-off at higher K shows how far the fresh cohort's ranking advantage extends.

results.feature_tested: refine_pattern re-grading demotes adversarial (spoofed high-score) patterns
results.pass_rule: After re-evaluation (herring claim=9.5, corrected=1.0; honest corrected=7.0), top-10 must contain ≤ 20% red herrings.

curves.classification_interpretation: Precision / recall / F1 treating 'honest pattern in top-10' as the positive class. F1 improvement = post - pre is the net gain from one round of refine_pattern re-grading. A positive delta indicates re-evaluation successfully identified and demoted the adversarial patterns; zero or negative indicates the backend cannot act on the corrected scores.

results.pass_rule: After re-evaluation (herring claim=8.0, corrected=3.0; honest corrected=7.0), top-10 must contain ≤ 20% red herrings.

A positive delta indicates re-evaluation successfully identified and demoted the adversarial patterns; zero or negative indicates the backend cannot act on the corrected scores.

summary.headline: medium-difficulty mean is the realistic workload estimate; easy is a feature-correctness check, hard is stress-test.
